articles.title: Royal Mail Wants Part-Time Workers to Help Deliver More Letters
articles.summary: Royal Mail, the UK postal service, is asking part-time postal workers to work more hours to help deliver letters on time. They have been criticized by the government and a regulator for not meeting delivery targets. For example, they are only delivering 75% of first-class letters on time, but the target is 93%. Royal Mail plans to invest £500 million over five years to improve things, like giving workers familiar routes and managing sickness. They will also deliver some lower-priority mail less often, but parcels will still come daily. The company says it needs to update its old rules for delivering mail. Workers have complained about hiding mail to meet targets, but the union supports the new plan if workers vote for it.

articles.title: A TV Star Found in Thailand
articles.summary: Jordan Wright was a man from Britain who was on TV shows like The Only Way Is Essex. He was found dead in a ditch in Thailand in March. A coroner in Essex is looking into what happened. His body was brought back to Essex. Friends from TV shows shared sad messages online. The coroner said sorry to his family. People are waiting for more tests to understand why he died. Jordan had been sharing fun videos from Thailand before he died.

articles.title: More People Steal Gas Because of War
articles.summary: A new report shows that more people are stealing gas from stations. The number of thefts has gone up by 62% compared to last year. This is happening because the war between the US, Israel, and Iran has made fuel prices higher. A gas station owner named Josh says he loses about £2,000 each week from people driving away without paying. He has five stations in England and sees about five thefts per week at each one. Videos show different people stealing gas at all times of day, like on a motorbike or in a van. The police say they are working to stop these crimes. Most thefts are planned, not because people are desperate. The average cost of each theft has also increased, making it a big problem for station owners.

articles.title: Fun Music Shows for Everyone in 2026
articles.summary: The BBC Proms is a big music festival that will happen in 2026. It will have 86 concerts in places like London and Bristol. There will be music from James Bond movies and prog rock, which is a type of rock music. Famous musicians like Dame Evelyn Glennie and Yuja Wang will play. The festival starts on July 17 and includes a tribute to Disney music and a space-themed show. Every concert will be on the radio and TV. There will also be special concerts for disabled musicians, like Felix Klieser who plays the horn with his feet. The festival celebrates different types of music for everyone to enjoy.

articles.title: Actor Patrick Muldoon Passes Away at 57
articles.summary: Patrick Muldoon, an American actor, has died at age 57. He was famous for playing roles in TV shows and movies. He acted in the soap opera Days of Our Lives and the sci-fi film Starship Troopers. His manager said he died at home from a heart attack. Friends and co-stars shared kind words about him, saying he was talented and nice. He also worked as a producer for films and had a band. He leaves behind family members who will miss him.

articles.title: Amy Winehouse's Dad Loses Court Case Over Auction
articles.summary: Amy Winehouse was a famous singer who died in 2011. Her dad, Mitch Winehouse, went to court because he thought two of Amy's friends, Naomi Parry and Catriona Gourlay, sold Amy's things at an auction without telling him. He wanted to stop them and get answers. The judge, Sarah Clarke, said Mitch lost the case. She said he could have found out about the items earlier if he tried harder. The judge also said Mitch cares a lot about protecting Amy's memory and making sure the family gets money from her things. Amy's friends said Amy gave them the items as gifts because she was very generous and didn't want to wear the same clothes twice. The court agreed with the friends, so Mitch did not win.
